title: Split XpertLab into Patient App and Staff App

requirements.summary: Restructure frontend into two distinct app entry points with separate route configurations and bundles
acceptanceCriteria: The Patient App loads and renders only patient-facing routes (/, /patient/*, /profile).
The Staff App loads and renders only staff-facing routes (/, /phlebotomist/*, /admin/*, /profile).
Neither app bundle imports or references the other app's page components.
Both apps share the same backend canister (no backend changes required).
Both apps use the same shared components (AppLayout, BottomNavigation, GradientButton, etc.) and design system.
The Patient App bottom navigation shows only patient nav items; the Staff App shows phlebotomist/admin nav items based on logged-in role.
Both apps load significantly faster than the combined single-bundle app.

file_operations.description: Create the Patient App root component with React Router configuration that includes only patient-facing routes: /, /patient/home, /patient/book-test, /patient/slot-selection, /patient/my-bookings, /patient/home-collection, /patient/my-home-collections, /patient/reports, /patient/my-vitals, /profile. Include auth guard and profile setup modal. Wire BottomNavigation with patient-only nav items from roleConfig.

Create the Staff App root component with React Router configuration that includes only staff-facing routes: /, /phlebotomist/tasks, /phlebotomist/home-collections, /phlebotomist/record-vitals, /phlebotomist/scan-camp-qr, /admin/bookings, /admin/reports, /admin/upload-report, /admin/incidents, /admin/audit-logs, /admin/create-camp, /staff/submit-incident, /profile. Include auth guard, profile setup modal, and role-based default redirect. Wire BottomNavigation with role-specific nav items (phlebotomist/labAdmin/superAdmin) from roleConfig.

Update roleConfig to export separate patient navigation items and staff navigation items (phlebotomist, labAdmin, superAdmin). Ensure BottomNavigation can consume either config based on the app context.

Update BottomNavigation to accept an optional navItems prop so it can render either patient or staff nav items depending on which app is consuming it. If no prop is provided, fall back to role-based config lookup.
Update the HTML shell to support dynamic script loading for either PatientApp or StaffApp based on a query parameter or route path. Ensure the title and favicon remain XpertLab branded.

Update the main entry point to conditionally render PatientApp or StaffApp based on the current URL path or query parameter. Wrap both with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider. Include logic to detect which app to load (e.g., /patient-app → PatientApp, /staff-app → StaffApp).

requirements.summary: Create Patient App login/landing screen with role validation and redirect logic
acceptanceCriteria: Patient App login screen displays XpertLab logo and branding.
Successful patient login redirects to /patient/home.
A non-patient role attempting to log in via the Patient App sees a clear error message and is not granted access.
Unauthenticated users are redirected to the login screen when accessing any patient route.

file_operations.description: Create a patient-specific login screen component displaying the XpertLab logo (use workspace file path frontend/public/assets/generated/xprtlab-logo.dim_256x256.png) and hero banner (use workspace file path frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1200x400.png), a tagline for patients, and an Internet Identity sign-in button. After authentication, fetch the caller's user profile. If the role is 'patient', redirect to /patient/home. If the role is phlebotomist/labAdmin/superAdmin, display a toast error: 'This app is for patients only. Please use the XpertLab Staff App.' and call logout. Use the useAuth hook and GradientButton component.

Wire PatientLoginScreen as the default route (/) in the Patient App router. Ensure the auth guard redirects unauthenticated users to / and authenticated non-patient users see the error message from PatientLoginScreen.

requirements.summary: Create Staff App login/landing screen with role-based redirect and patient exclusion
acceptanceCriteria: Staff App login screen displays XpertLab Staff branding.
Phlebotomist login redirects to /phlebotomist/tasks.
Lab Admin login redirects to /admin/bookings.
Super Admin login redirects to /admin/audit-logs.
A patient-role user attempting to log in via the Staff App sees a clear error message and cannot proceed.
Unauthenticated users are redirected to the staff login screen when accessing any staff route.

file_operations.description: Create a staff-specific login screen component displaying the XpertLab Staff logo (use workspace file path frontend/public/assets/generated/xprtlab-logo.dim_256x256.png) and branding, a tagline for staff, and an Internet Identity sign-in button. After authentication, fetch the caller's user profile. If the role is 'patient', display a toast error: 'This app is for staff only. Please use the XpertLab Patient App.' and call logout. If the role is phlebotomist, redirect to /phlebotomist/tasks. If labAdmin, redirect to /admin/bookings. If superAdmin, redirect to /admin/audit-logs. Use the useAuth hook, GradientButton component, and getDefaultPathForRole from roleHelpers.

Wire StaffLoginScreen as the default route (/) in the Staff App router. Ensure the auth guard redirects unauthenticated users to / and authenticated patient users see the error message from StaffLoginScreen.

requirements.summary: Add a landing/selector page at the root URL with Patient App and Staff App selection cards
acceptanceCriteria: The root URL (/) displays a full-screen landing page with two app selection cards.
The 'Patient App' card navigates to the patient login flow.
The 'Staff App' card navigates to the staff login flow.
The landing page uses the XpertLab gradient branding (#0D47A1 → #26C6DA).
Already-authenticated users bypass the landing page and are redirected directly to their role's home screen.

file_operations.description: Create a full-screen landing page component with XpertLab branding (use workspace file path frontend/public/assets/generated/xprtlab-logo.dim_256x256.png) and hero banner (use workspace file path frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1200x400.png). Display two large, centered cards: 'Patient App' with description 'Book tests, manage bookings, view reports' and a GradientButton that navigates to /patient-app, and 'Staff App' with description 'Manage tasks, upload reports, view audit logs' and a GradientButton that navigates to /staff-app. Apply the XpertLab gradient background (from #0D47A1 to #26C6DA). Use TanStack Router's useNavigate for navigation.

Update the main entry point to render AppSelectorPage at the root URL (/). If the user is already authenticated, check their role and redirect directly to their role's default path using getDefaultPathForRole. Ensure /patient-app loads PatientApp and /staff-app loads StaffApp.

Update the existing App.tsx to register the AppSelectorPage route at / and ensure /patient-app and /staff-app routes are registered to load PatientApp and StaffApp respectively. Wire the auth guard to redirect authenticated users from / to their role's default path.
